# UC-22.46.5 · Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training

## Description

Prevents the 'developers with production access but stale training' anti-pattern that surfaces in GDPR Art.32 enforcement cases.

## Value

Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.

## Implementation

(1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window. Indexes required: `index=lms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=completion`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user group scope last_completion
```

Understanding this SPL

**Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training** — Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.

Documented **Data sources**: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Filters the current rows with `where scope="regulated"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: group, last_completion, scope, user, user].

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'.

## SPL

```spl
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user group scope last_completion
```

## Visualization

Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'. Key fields: `group`, `last_completion`, `scope`, `user`, `user]`.
